root_text.sn40.9:1.4: ‘idha bhikkhu sabbanimittānaṁ amanasikārā animittaṁ cetosamādhiṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.sn40.9:1.6: So khvāhaṁ, āvuso, sabbanimittānaṁ amanasikārā animittaṁ cetosamādhiṁ upasampajja viharāmi.
root_text.sn40.9:1.7: Tassa mayhaṁ, āvuso, iminā vihārena viharato nimittānusāri viññāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn40.9:2.1: Atha kho maṁ, āvuso, bhagavā iddhiyā upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca:
root_text.sn40.9:2.3: Mā, brāhmaṇa, animittaṁ cetosamādhiṁ pamādo, animitte cetosamādhismiṁ cittaṁ saṇṭhapehi, animitte cetosamādhismiṁ cittaṁ ekodiṁ karohi, animitte cetosamādhismiṁ cittaṁ samādahā’ti.
root_text.sn40.9:2.4: So khvāhaṁ, āvuso, aparena samayena sabbanimittānaṁ amanasikārā animittaṁ cetosamādhiṁ upasampajja vihāsiṁ.
root_text.sn40.9:2.5: Yañhi taṁ, āvuso, sammā vadamāno vadeyya:
root_text.sn40.9:2.6: ‘satthārānuggahito sāvako mahābhiññataṁ patto’ti, mamaṁ taṁ sammā vadamāno vadeyya:

translation_text.sn40.9:1.1: “They speak of this thing called the ‘signless immersion of the heart’.
translation_text.sn40.9:1.2: What is the signless immersion of the heart?
translation_text.sn40.9:1.4: ‘It’s when a mendicant, not focusing on any signs, enters and remains in the signless immersion of the heart.
translation_text.sn40.9:1.5: This is called the signless immersion of the heart.’
translation_text.sn40.9:1.6: And so … I was entering and remaining in the signless immersion of the heart.
translation_text.sn40.9:1.7: While I was in that meditation, my consciousness followed after signs.
translation_text.sn40.9:2.1: Then the Buddha came up to me with his psychic power and said,
translation_text.sn40.9:2.3: Don’t neglect the signless immersion of the heart, brahmin! Settle your mind in the signless immersion of the heart; unify your mind and immerse it in the signless immersion of the heart.’
translation_text.sn40.9:2.4: And so, after some time … I entered and remained in the signless immersion of the heart.
translation_text.sn40.9:2.5: So if anyone should be rightly called
translation_text.sn40.9:2.6: a disciple who attained to great direct knowledge with help from the Teacher, it’s me.”
